message:
feat: add core module implementations for session model, discovery, and errors

Continue the casr project scaffold with concrete module implementations:

- src/model.rs: canonical session intermediate representation types
  (CanonicalSession, CanonicalMessage, MessageRole) enabling lossless
  cross-provider session conversion
- src/discovery.rs: provider detection and session lookup logic for
  auto-detecting which provider originated a given session
- src/error.rs: typed error hierarchy using thiserror for clear error
  reporting across the detect-read-convert-write pipeline
- Update beads tracker with task progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,8 +42,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-2rl.2","title":"Define Provider trait with read/write/detect methods","description":"Define the Provider trait in `src/providers/mod.rs`. This is the core abstraction that each provider (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and future providers) implements.

## Trait Surface

```rust
pub trait Provider {
    /// Human-readable name (e.g. "Claude Code")
    fn name(&self) -> &str;

    /// Stable slug used in logs/metadata (e.g. "claude-code", "codex", "gemini")
    fn slug(&self) -> &str;

    /// CLI alias used in `casr <alias> resume ...` (e.g. "cc", "cod", "gmi")
    fn cli_alias(&self) -> &str;

    /// Detect presence of the provider binary + session roots.
    /// This MUST be evidence-rich (not a single bool).
    fn detect(&self) -> DetectionResult;

    /// Candidate session roots for this provider (respects *_HOME overrides).
    fn session_roots(&self) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Fast ownership check: return the native session file path if this provider owns `session_id`.
    /// Should avoid full file parsing when possible.
    fn owns_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Read a session from native format into canonical IR.
    fn read_session(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<CanonicalSession>;

    /// Write a canonical session into this provider's native format.
    /// Returns all paths written + generated session_id.
    fn write_session(&self, session: &CanonicalSession, opts: &WriteOptions)
        -> anyhow::Result<WrittenSession>;

    /// The resume command string for this provider (e.g. `claude --resume <id>`).
    fn resume_command(&self, session_id: &str) -> String;
}
```

Also define:

```rust
pub struct DetectionResult {
    pub binary: Option<BinaryEvidence>,
    pub roots: Vec<RootEvidence>,
    pub notes: Vec<String>,
}

pub struct BinaryEvidence {
    pub exe: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub version: Option<String>,
}

pub struct RootEvidence {
    pub path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub exists: bool,
    pub readable: bool,
    pub writable: bool,
    pub evidence: Vec<String>,
}

impl DetectionResult {
    pub fn can_read(&self) -> bool { /* any readable root */ }
    pub fn can_write(&self) -> bool { /* any writable root OR creatable root */ }
    pub fn can_resume(&self) -> bool { self.binary.is_some() }
}

pub struct WrittenSession {
    pub paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
    pub session_id: String,
    pub resume_command: String,
    pub backup_paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub struct WriteOptions {
    pub force: bool,
}
```

## Detection Semantics (Critical For UX)

- `detect()` MUST distinguish:
  - binary present vs missing (`can_resume()`),
  - session roots present/readable (`can_read()`),
  - session roots writable/creatable (`can_write()`).
- casr should be able to READ sessions even when the provider binary is not installed.
- casr should be able to WRITE sessions as long as roots are writable/creatable, even if the binary is missing (warn in UX).

## Conflict + Backup Policy

- Conflict/`--force` handling MUST live in exactly one place: `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3).
- Provider writers call `atomic_write`; the pipeline should not duplicate pre-checks (avoid TOCTOU).
- `WrittenSession.backup_paths` is surfaced to UX so users can find restored/overwritten files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Provider` is object-safe and storable as `Box<dyn Provider>` in a registry.
- `DetectionResult` is rich enough to power `casr providers` output (binary evidence + root evidence + notes).
- Env var overrides are supported for root derivation: `CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`.
- `write_session()` uses `WriteOptions.force` and surfaces backup paths when overwriting.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:32.295047628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:48:08.506007209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.241171305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.800231584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.3","title":"Implement helper functions for content normalization","description":"Port/adapt the shared normalization utilities from CASS connector helpers. These are used by all readers and writers and must be centralized.

## Helpers

1. `flatten_content(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String`

Handles content representations across providers:
- string -> string
- array of blocks -> concatenate extracted parts
  - `{type:"text", text:"..."}` and `{type:"input_text", text:"..."}` contribute text
  - `{type:"tool_use", name:"...", input:{...}}` contributes a stable human-readable marker like `[Tool: name - <description>]`
  - mixed arrays (text + tool_use + strings) must be supported
- object with `text` field -> text
- null/number/bool/unexpected -> empty

2. `parse_timestamp(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<i64>`

Parse timestamps into epoch millis:
- i64 seconds vs millis heuristic (critical for correctness)
- numeric strings and float strings
- ISO-8601/RFC3339 with offsets, with/without fractional seconds
- NEVER panics; returns None on unparseable input

3. `reindex_messages(messages: &mut Vec<CanonicalMessage>)`

After filtering/sorting, reassign `idx` to 0..N-1.

4. `truncate_title(text: &str, max_len: usize) -> String`

Title extraction: first line, trimmed, truncated with `...`.

5. `normalize_role(role_str: &str) -> MessageRole`

Case-insensitive mapping:
- user -> User
- assistant/model/agent -> Assistant
- tool -> Tool
- system -> System
- other -> Other(role)

## Tool Call/Result Extraction (Best-Effort)

In addition to `flatten_content`, add best-effort helpers to extract structured tool data when present in native schemas:
- `extract_tool_calls(val) -> Vec<ToolCall>` from content blocks like `{type:"tool_use", name, input}`
- `extract_tool_results(val) -> Vec<ToolResult>` where providers encode results in-message

When extraction is not possible, preserve raw provider fields in `message.extra`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Helpers match CASS behavior for timestamp parsing + content flattening (parity tests in bd-24z.10).
- Helper tests cover edge cases exhaustively (bd-24z.7) and include detailed failure logs.
- No helper ever panics on malformed provider data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:43.396341180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:51:58.128848583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.880037435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.868053890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zw","title":"EPIC: Project Bootstrap & Scaffold","description":"Set up the casr (Cross Agent Session Resumer) Rust project from scratch. This is a CLI tool that allows users to resume AI coding agent sessions from one provider using a different provider. E.g., `casr cc resume <codex-session-id>` would let Claude Code resume a session originally created by Codex. The tool auto-detects the originating provider, converts through a canonical intermediate format, and writes a valid session file in the target provider's native format. Binary name: `casr`.

Covers: Cargo.toml, directory structure, AGENTS.md, rust-toolchain.toml, .gitignore, CI scaffolding, and the initial module skeleton.

## Success Criteria

- Repo builds cleanly under Rust nightly (edition 2024): `cargo check --all-targets`.
- Base CI workflow exists and runs at least fmt/clippy/test (can be expanded later).
- Module skeleton exists with clear boundaries (model/providers/discovery/pipeline/errors).
- Guardrails for CASS vendoring and no-runtime-dependency are documented and testable.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:35.788994132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:52.382127741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","infrastructure"]}
-{"id":"bd-2zw.1","title":"Initialize Cargo project with proper structure","description":"Run `cargo init` or manually create `Cargo.toml` for the casr project.

Key settings:
- name=`casr`
- edition=`2024`
- rust-toolchain nightly
- dependencies: `clap` (derive), `serde`/`serde_json`, `anyhow`, `thiserror`, `chrono`, `colored`, `walkdir`, `glob`, `dirs`, `uuid`, `which`
- release profile: `opt-level='z'`, `lto=true`, `codegen-units=1`, `panic='abort'`, `strip=true`

Create:
- `rust-toolchain.toml`
- `.gitignore` (`/target`, editor temp files, OS noise)
- `build.rs` with `vergen-gix` for build metadata

Version output requirement:
- `casr --version` includes version + git commit + build date + target triple.

CASS Independence Policy (MANDATORY):
1. casr MUST NOT add `coding_agent_session_search` (CASS) as a runtime dependency (no git/path/crates linkage).
2. We may copy/adapt relevant connector logic into casr source code (vendoring), but all code must build and run with casr alone.
3. Preserve attribution and MIT license compliance for copied/adapted snippets.
4. Add a short `docs/cass-porting-notes.md` documenting:
   - source files copied/adapted,
   - behavior deltas introduced in casr,
   - the rationale for no-runtime-dependency architecture.

NOTE: Additional crates are added by later tasks — tracing/tracing-subscriber (bd-2zw.6), tempfile (tests), assert_cmd/predicates (e2e tests), clap_complete (shell completions).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `cargo check --all-targets` succeeds after scaffold (even with TODO stubs).
- Crate roots include `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Release profile matches the size-optimized settings above.
- `casr --version` prints vergen-gix build metadata.
- `.gitignore` prevents committing build artifacts and local caches.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:41.026548622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:25:24.207357968Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:58:17.031194185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2zw.2","title":"Create module skeleton with proper file layout","description":"Create the initial Rust module structure.

Files to create:
- `src/main.rs` (entry point, clap CLI dispatch)
- `src/lib.rs` (public API)
- `src/model.rs` (canonical session types)
- `src/providers/mod.rs` (provider trait + wiring)
- `src/providers/claude_code.rs` (reader+writer stub)
- `src/providers/codex.rs` (reader+writer stub)
- `src/providers/gemini.rs` (reader+writer stub)
- `src/discovery.rs` (provider detection + session lookup)
- `src/pipeline.rs` (conversion orchestration)
- `src/error.rs` (typed errors)

Each file starts with a module doc comment explaining its purpose and a minimal skeleton (pub struct/trait/fn with `todo!()` where appropriate). The module tree should compile with `cargo check` even if everything is unimplemented.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `cargo check --all-targets` passes with the skeleton in place.
- Public module boundaries match the "Key Files" list in `AGENTS.md`.
- Each module has a short top-level doc comment describing its responsibility.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:47.953059288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:44.312692341Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.640829009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:20.398877633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2zw.3","title":"Write AGENTS.md adapted for casr project","description":"Adapt the dcg AGENTS.md for the casr project. Keep the universal rules (Rule 0 override, Rule 1 no file deletion, no destructive git commands, Rust/Cargo toolchain, code editing discipline, no file proliferation, no backwards compatibility concerns, compiler checks, br/bv workflow, session completion protocol, multi-agent coexistence). Replace all dcg-specific content with casr-specific content: architecture overview (detect->read->canonical->write pipeline), key files table, provider system description, testing strategy, CLI reference. Remove dcg hook protocol, pattern system, allowlist, and other dcg-specific sections. Add casr-specific sections: canonical session format reference, provider format specs, round-trip testing invariants, adding new providers guide.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:01.719667755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:02:06.329536255Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:02:06.329513913Z","close_reason":"Completed: AGENTS.md rewritten from dcg-specific content to casr-specific architecture/protocol while preserving shared rules/tooling.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:14.294193372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:20.472850211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zw.4","title":"Set up GitHub Actions CI workflow","description":"Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with staged but comprehensive jobs.

Required jobs:
1. `check`
   - `cargo fmt --check`
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo test`
2. `coverage`
   - `cargo llvm-cov`
   - enforce thresholds for overall + critical modules (model, pipeline)
3. `parity`
   - run CASS parity regression suite for readers
4. `conformance`
   - run writer golden-output conformance suite
5. `roundtrip`
   - run cross-provider fidelity matrix tests
6. `contracts`
   - run JSON schema/contract tests for `--json` outputs
7. `e2e`
   - run `scripts/e2e_test.sh` with isolated provider homes
8. `smoke-real` (opt-in)
   - run real-provider acceptance harness when provider binaries/homes are available
9. `perf-regression`
   - run targeted discovery/reader/writer perf regression checks and enforce documented budgets

Workflow requirements:
- Use `actions/checkout`, nightly toolchain, and Rust cache.
- Emit artifacts on failure (logs, generated outputs, diffs, coverage reports, schema diffs, perf metrics).
- Provide clear job summaries with first failing assertion location.
- Keep a secrets-free path for local reproducibility.
- Explicitly mark required vs optional jobs (no silent skipping).

Implementation strategy:
- Start with `check` + `coverage`, then incrementally enable parity/conformance/roundtrip/e2e/perf as tasks land.
- Preserve deterministic output paths so failed artifacts are easy to compare across runs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- CI includes parity, conformance, roundtrip, contracts, e2e, and perf jobs with artifact upload.
- Failure output is actionable without re-running immediately.
- Optional real-provider smoke job is explicit and non-silent when skipped.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:07.872512054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:53:04.956645291Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:14.087396285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:20.544706508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zw.5","title":"Implement error types with actionable messages","description":"Define the error type hierarchy in `src/error.rs` using `thiserror`. Errors must be actionable: every error should tell the user what to do next.

## Error Types

- `SessionNotFound { session_id, providers_checked, roots_scanned, file_counts }`
  - Example: `Session abc123 not found. Checked: Claude Code (roots: 2, files: 23), Codex (roots: 1, files: 156). Run 'casr list' to see sessions.`

- `AmbiguousSessionId { session_id, candidates: Vec<Candidate> }`
  - Returned when multiple providers match a user-supplied ID.
  - Must include provider slug + resolved path per candidate.
  - Must include remediation: `--source <alias>` or `--source <path>`.

- `UnknownProviderAlias { alias, known_aliases }`
  - Example: `Unknown provider alias xyz. Known: cc (Claude Code), cod (Codex), gmi (Gemini).`

- `ProviderUnavailable { provider, reason, evidence }`
  - Use when the requested provider cannot be used for the operation.
  - Reasons should distinguish:
    - binary missing (cannot resume, but may still read/write),
    - no readable roots (cannot read),
    - no writable/creatable roots (cannot write),
    - permission denied / read-only root.

- `SessionReadError { path, provider, detail }`
  - Example: `Failed to read Codex session at ...: invalid JSON on line 42`.

- `SessionWriteError { path, provider, detail }`
  - Example: `Failed to write Claude Code session to ...: permission denied`.

- `SessionConflict { session_id, existing_path }`
  - Example: `Session already exists in Claude Code at ... Use --force to overwrite (creates .bak).`

- `ValidationError { errors, warnings, info }`
  - Error variant used when conversion must stop; warnings/info are still surfaced in UX/JSON.

- `VerifyFailed { provider, written_paths, detail }`
  - Used when read-back verification fails. This is a casr bug (writers/pipeline).

## Library vs CLI

- Use `anyhow` for internal propagation.
- Use `thiserror` for the public error surface and for stable `--json` `error_type` strings.
- The CLI layer renders errors with colors and remediation hints; `--json` outputs the structured envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Error types cover: not found, ambiguous, unreadable, unwritable, conflict, read/write/verify failures.
- `AmbiguousSessionId` is implemented and propagated from discovery/pipeline.
- `ProviderUnavailable` can represent "binary missing but roots writable" as a WARNING in UX (not a hard error unless write is impossible).
- Error rendering is stable and covered by JSON contract tests (bd-24z.11).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:23.335783079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:48:43.889323660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","errors"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:12.256540775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:27.355019002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+//! Actionable typed errors for casr.
+//!
+//! Each error variant includes enough context for the user to understand
+//! what went wrong and what to do next. Internal propagation uses `anyhow`;
+//! the public API exposes these `thiserror` types.
+
+use std::path::PathBuf;
+
+/// Errors that casr surfaces to the user.
+#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
+pub enum CasrError {
+    /// Session ID not found in any installed provider.
+    #[error(
+        "Session '{session_id}' not found. Checked: {providers_checked:?} ({sessions_scanned} sessions scanned). Run 'casr list' to see all sessions."
+    )]
+    SessionNotFound {
+        session_id: String,
+        providers_checked: Vec<String>,
+        sessions_scanned: usize,
+    },
+
+    /// Target provider binary/directory not detected on this machine.
+    #[error("{provider} is not installed. {install_hint}")]
+    ProviderNotInstalled {
+        provider: String,
+        install_hint: String,
+    },
+
+    /// Unknown provider alias in CLI input.
+    #[error("Unknown provider alias '{alias}'. Known aliases: {}", known_aliases.join(", "))]
+    ProviderNotDetected {
+        alias: String,
+        known_aliases: Vec<String>,
+    },
+
+    /// Failed to parse a session from its native format.
+    #[error("Failed to read {provider} session at {}: {detail}", path.display())]
+    SessionReadError {
+        path: PathBuf,
+        provider: String,
+        detail: String,
+    },
+
+    /// Failed to write a converted session to disk.
+    #[error("Failed to write {provider} session to {}: {detail}", path.display())]
+    SessionWriteError {
+        path: PathBuf,
+        provider: String,
+        detail: String,
+    },
+
+    /// Target session file already exists and `--force` was not supplied.
+    #[error(
+        "Session already exists at {}. Use --force to overwrite (creates .bak backup).",
+        existing_path.display()
+    )]
+    SessionConflict {
+        session_id: String,
+        existing_path: PathBuf,
+    },
+
+    /// Canonical session failed validation checks.
+    #[error("Session validation failed: {}", errors.join("; "))]
+    ValidationError { errors: Vec<String> },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+//! Canonical session model — the IR (intermediate representation) for casr.
+//!
+//! Every provider's native format is parsed into these types, and every
+//! target format is generated from them. This is the Rosetta Stone of
+//! cross-provider session conversion.
+
+use std::path::PathBuf;
+
+use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Core types
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/// A provider-agnostic representation of an AI coding agent session.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct CanonicalSession {
+    /// Unique session identifier (provider-assigned or generated).
+    pub session_id: String,
+    /// Provider slug that originally created this session (e.g. `"claude-code"`).
+    pub provider_slug: String,
+    /// Project root directory, if known.
+    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,
+    /// Human-readable title (first user message or explicit title).
+    pub title: Option<String>,
+    /// Session start time as epoch milliseconds.
+    pub started_at: Option<i64>,
+    /// Session end time as epoch milliseconds.
+    pub ended_at: Option<i64>,
+    /// Ordered conversation messages.
+    pub messages: Vec<CanonicalMessage>,
+    /// Provider-specific extras that don't map to canonical fields.
+    pub metadata: serde_json::Value,
+    /// Filesystem path of the original session file.
+    pub source_path: PathBuf,
+    /// Convenience: most common model name in the session.
+    pub model_name: Option<String>,
+}
+
+/// A single message in a canonical session.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct CanonicalMessage {
+    /// Zero-based sequential index.
+    pub idx: usize,
+    /// Who sent this message.
+    pub role: MessageRole,
+    /// The textual content of the message.
+    pub content: String,
+    /// Message timestamp as epoch milliseconds.
+    pub timestamp: Option<i64>,
+    /// Model name or `"user"` or `"reasoning"`.
+    pub author: Option<String>,
+    /// Tool invocations made in this message.
+    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>,
+    /// Results returned from tool invocations.
+    pub tool_results: Vec<ToolResult>,
+    /// Provider-specific fields preserved for round-trip fidelity.
+    pub extra: serde_json::Value,
+}
+
+/// The role of a message sender.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub enum MessageRole {
+    User,
+    Assistant,
+    Tool,
+    System,
+    Other(String),
+}
+
+/// A tool invocation within a message.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct ToolCall {
+    pub id: Option<String>,
+    pub name: String,
+    pub arguments: serde_json::Value,
+}
+
+/// A tool result within a message.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct ToolResult {
+    pub call_id: Option<String>,
+    pub content: String,
+    pub is_error: bool,
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Helpers (implemented in bd-2rl.3)
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/// Flatten heterogeneous content representations into a single string.
+pub fn flatten_content(_value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
+    todo!("bd-2rl.3: flatten_content")
+}
+
+/// Parse a timestamp value (ISO-8601, epoch seconds, epoch millis) into epoch millis.
+pub fn parse_timestamp(_value: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<i64> {
+    todo!("bd-2rl.3: parse_timestamp")
+}
+
+/// Re-assign sequential idx values (0, 1, 2, …) after filtering/sorting.
+pub fn reindex_messages(_messages: &mut Vec<CanonicalMessage>) {
+    todo!("bd-2rl.3: reindex_messages")
+}
+
+/// Extract a title from message content (first line, truncated).
+pub fn truncate_title(_text: &str, _max_len: usize) -> String {
+    todo!("bd-2rl.3: truncate_title")
+}
+
+/// Map provider-specific role strings to canonical [`MessageRole`].
+pub fn normalize_role(_role_str: &str) -> MessageRole {
+    todo!("bd-2rl.3: normalize_role")
+}
